scenario.context: Station 04: evaluate cost, security, and sustainable requirements.
scenario.mission: You prepare a decision that balances cost, protection needs, and long-term operations.

constraints: Do not look only at purchase price
Assess operational consequences over the long term

customerRequest.subject: Station 04: Evaluation matrix
customerRequest.body: Please prepare a brief decision basis that summarizes cost efficiency, security, and sustainability.

questions.title: Explain the trade-off
questions.prompt: Describe in 4-6 sentences how you would assess cost, security, and sustainability together for this network project.
questions.expectedOutline: Life-cycle-oriented trade-off with protection needs and operational impact.

feedback.correct: Correct: The trade-off combines economics and protection in a coherent way.
feedback.wrong: Please bring the life cycle, protection needs, and sustainability together more clearly.

questions.title: Security-relevant procurement
questions.prompt: Which decision is most sensible for an administrative environment with personal data?

options.text: Cheapest possible hardware without a central patching and monitoring concept
options.rationale: Saves money in the short term, but increases operational risk.

options.text: Focus only on maximum performance and clarify protection and operations later
options.rationale: Performance alone does not cover protection needs.

options.text: Choose components that combine security functions, central management, and traceable operating costs
options.rationale: This sensibly combines protection needs, cost efficiency, and maintainability.

feedback.correct: Correct: The selection is both secure and operationally viable.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: The decision must consider protection needs and operating costs together.

questions.title: Recognize sustainability criteria
questions.prompt: Which points help to realistically consider sustainability in network planning? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Assess energy demand over the service life
options.rationale: Affects operating costs and environmental impact.

options.text: Check the expandability and repairability of components
options.rationale: Extends useful life and reduces replacement needs.

options.text: Only look at the color of the housing

options.rationale: Can reduce material and energy use.

options.text: Remove security functions to save power
options.rationale: That would not be a viable sustainability strategy.

feedback.correct: Correct: Sustainability comes from a life-cycle and operational perspective.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: Please weigh energy, service life, and consolidation more strongly.

questions.title: Annual electricity cost difference
questions.prompt: An optimized solution reduces power consumption by 180 W. At 2500 operating hours per year and 0.31 EUR per kWh: What is the annual savings in EUR, rounded?

feedback.wrong: Please combine power, operating hours, and electricity price correctly.

questions.title: Build decision memo
questions.prompt: Put the steps for a robust decision memo in order.
items: Name protection needs and operational requirements
Estimate costs over the service life
correctOrder: Name protection needs and operational requirements
Estimate costs over the service life

feedback.correct: Correct: The decision memo leads to the recommendation in a structured way.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please clarify requirements and costs first, then compare options.
